title: Kubota Garden — Park Review | Condé Nast Traveler
content: Twenty acres of gorgeous Japanese gardens on Seattle's Southside. At Kubota Garden, 20 acres of gorgeous Japanese gardens await visitors willing to make the trip to the Rainier Beach neighborhood in South Seattle. First started in 1927 by Fujitaro Kubota, the complex is now part of Seattle’s public park system. **What's the best way to experience it?**. While some tiered paths and small bridges may present challenges for disabled visitors, the majority of the garden is simple to move around and accessible to everyone. **All said and done, what—and who—is this best for?**. $$$ | United States, Seattle, 99 Union St. By most any measure, Seattle’s top hotel. $$ | United States, Seattle, 411 University St. Built in the 1920s, this massive Italian Renaissance hotel is breathtakingly opulent. $ | United States, Seattle, 110 Stewart St. An architecture and design lover's dream. $$ | United States, Seattle, 2000 4th Ave. Belltown is back, and this Scandinavian-chic stay is a sanctuary from the downtown core.
